The incredibly massive, deeply humiliating hostage crisis in southern India aggressively continued to paralyze the national security apparatus. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On November 12, 1927, Leon Trotsky, the incredibly massive, brilliant Marxist theoretician and absolutely foundational architect of the massive Soviet Red Army, suffered an incredibly massive, devastating political defeat. Following incredibly fierce, brutal internal power struggles after the death of Lenin, the incredibly ruthless Joseph Stalin successfully and aggressively expelled Trotsky from the massive Soviet Communist Party. This massive, highly strategic purge absolutely cemented Stalin's terrifying, absolute dictatorial control, fundamentally sending Trotsky into a massive, desperate global exile that ended in his brutal assassination.

On November 12, 1948, Hideki Tojo, the incredibly massive, heavily authoritarian former Prime Minister and absolute, unyielding military dictator of Imperial Japan during World War II, was officially and aggressively sentenced to death. The massive International Military Tribunal for the Far East heavily convicted him of incredibly massive, horrific war crimes and absolute crimes against humanity. His incredibly massive, highly publicized trial and subsequent execution by hanging absolutely and fundamentally destroyed the horrific, brutal legacy of Japanese militaristic imperialism.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On November 12, 1896, Salim Ali, the incredibly massive, universally revered, and absolute foundational "Birdman of India," was officially born in Bombay (Mumbai). He achieved absolute, historical immortality by heavily conducting incredibly massive, highly systematic, and fiercely pioneering surveys of bird populations across the entire massive Indian subcontinent. His incredibly massive, brilliant ornithological research and deeply passionate conservation advocacy heavily established the massive Bombay Natural History Society, fundamentally laying the absolute architectural bedrock for modern Indian ecological science.

On November 12, 1930, the incredibly massive, deeply historic, and fiercely pivotal First Round Table Conference officially and aggressively commenced in London. Heavily organized by the British government to discuss incredibly massive, profound constitutional reforms in India, the massive gathering was fiercely and notably boycotted by the Indian National Congress, who were aggressively leading the massive Civil Disobedience Movement. This incredible, massive political absence fundamentally rendered the massive conference largely ineffective, deeply forcing the British to aggressively seek negotiations with Mahatma Gandhi for subsequent summits.
